sense.definition: a situation in which something fails to work properly, especially because of a mechanical failure

examples.x: The car had a breakdown on the highway, causing traffic delays.
A sudden breakdown in the elevator left passengers stranded.
The factory halted production due to a machinery breakdown.
He called for roadside assistance after experiencing an engine breakdown.
Frequent breakdowns in the power grid led to widespread blackouts.

sense.definition: (of a machine or vehicle) to stop working as a result of a malfunction

examples.x: The machinery broke down during the production process, leading to delays.
The old refrigerator broke down, and we had to replace it.
The printer suddenly broke down, causing a delay in printing documents.
The car broke down on the highway due to engine trouble.
The lawnmower broke down in the middle of mowing the lawn.

sense.definition: a loud, sudden explosion of unburned fuel in the engine or exhaust system

examples.x: The old car occasionally experienced a backfire when starting up.
She heard a backfire from the exhaust after accelerating.
The mechanic diagnosed the backfire as a result of a faulty spark plug.
They adjusted the timing to prevent engine backfires.
A backfire could indicate issues with fuel mixture or ignition timing.

sense.definition: (of a vehicle or engine) to fail to ignite properly or skip a beat during operation

examples.x: My car misfires occasionally, especially when it's cold outside.
The engine misfires if I don't maintain it regularly.
The mechanic is fixing the issue that causes the engine to misfire.
Last week, my motorcycle misfired on the way to work, causing me to be late.
While I was driving home yesterday, the engine was misfiring continuously.

sense.definition: (of a vehicle or engine) to stop suddenly and unexpectedly, especially because of a lack of fuel or mechanical issues
a sudden and serious failure of a part or device, leading to immediate malfunction or stoppage
a small hole or tear in a tire caused by a sharp object
an extra tire kept in a vehicle for use in case one of the main tires becomes flat or damaged

examples.x: He pulled over to change the flat tire with the spare tire from the trunk.
The rental car came equipped with a compact spare tire for emergencies.
It's important to check the air pressure of the spare tire regularly to ensure it's ready for use.
She used the jack and lug wrench to lower the spare tire from underneath the truck.
He stored an emergency kit with tools and a flashlight near the spare tire in the trunk.

sense.definition: a mechanic or someone who works on cars

examples.x: He's a talented grease monkey who can fix any engine problem.
The grease monkey inspected the brakes for wear.
They hired a skilled grease monkey to restore the classic car.
Being a grease monkey requires knowledge of automotive systems.
She became known as the neighborhood grease monkey for her car repair skills.

sense.definition: to fix something that is damaged, broken, or not working properly

examples.x: Can you help me repair this torn book page with tape?
He knows how to repair bicycles, so he fixed mine.
She learned how to repair the flat tire on her bicycle.
The mechanic will repair my car's engine tomorrow.
The workshop can repair the broken furniture.

sense.definition: the routine act of inspection and maintenance of a machine or vehicle in order to keep it working
a special road feature designed to stop trucks that have lost control due to brake failure or other reasons
a car that has been modified for high speed and performance

examples.x: He spent years building his dream hot rod in the garage.
The hot rod raced down the track with impressive acceleration.
Building a hot rod involves modifying and tuning the engine.
They customized the hot rod with a powerful engine and custom paint job.
She admired the sleek design of the vintage hot rod at the car show.

sense.definition: a mechanical device for lifting heavy objects or vehicles
to raise a vehicle off the ground using a jack

examples.x: He jacked up the car to change the oil.
She needed to jack up the truck to inspect the suspension.
They carefully jacked up the car before rotating the tires.
Always use safety precautions when jacking up a car.

sense.definition: a tool for loosening or tightening the nuts that hold a wheel in place on a car

examples.x: He used a lug wrench to loosen the lug nuts before changing the tire.
She kept a lug wrench in the trunk for emergencies.
They upgraded to a heavy-duty lug wrench for easier use.
A lug wrench is essential for changing flat tires.
After getting a flat tire, Jack used a lug wrench to change the wheel.

sense.definition: the outer layer of paint applied to a surface, such as a vehicle or a building
the process of constructing, repairing, or restoring the exterior panels and structural components of a vehicle

examples.x: The accident required extensive bodywork.
She took her car in for expert bodywork.
He did bodywork on his motorcycle all weekend.
Rust spots on the fenders needed bodywork.
The bodywork included frame straightening and repainting.

sense.definition: to apply a new layer of paint to something that already has paint on it
the practice of changing the position of a vehicle's tires to ensure even wear and prolong their lifespan
the adjustment of the angles of the wheels to ensure they are perpendicular to the ground and parallel to each other

examples.x: Proper wheel alignment helps vehicles maintain stability and ensures even tire wear over time.
Mechanics use special tools to measure and adjust wheel alignment accurately.
Incorrect wheel alignment can cause steering problems and uneven tire tread, leading to increased fuel consumption.
It's recommended to check the wheel alignment regularly, especially after hitting a curb or driving over rough terrain.
Car manufacturers provide specifications for wheel alignment to ensure optimal performance and safety on the road.

sense.definition: a place where vehicles are cleaned using water and cleaning products

examples.x: I took my car to the car wash to get rid of the dirt and mud from the weekend trip.
The car wash near my office offers different packages, including exterior wash and interior cleaning.
It's important to choose a reliable car wash to avoid any damage to your vehicle's paint.
At the car wash, they use specialized equipment to ensure every part of the car gets thoroughly cleaned.
After the car wash, my car looked shiny and new, much to my satisfaction.

sense.definition: to thoroughly clean or decorate something, paying attention to small or specific aspects

examples.x: Every weekend, she details her car meticulously, ensuring it shines brightly.
He spent the entire afternoon detailing his car, polishing every surface and vacuuming the interior thoroughly.
The auto shop offers a service where professionals detail your car, leaving it spotless inside and out.
She decided to detail her car before putting it up for sale to make it more appealing to potential buyers.

sense.definition: to clean and polish a car meticulously

examples.x: He valets cars at a detailing shop every weekend.
She is valeting a classic car for a car show next week.
Last month, he valeted a vintage car for a collector.
Next year, she will valet cars at a luxury car dealership.
They have valeted hundreds of cars to perfection since they opened their business.

sense.definition: a vehicle that is in poor condition, typically used for rough or off-road driving

examples.x: The old beater they bought for a few hundred dollars got them through their road trip without any issues.
He's looking to sell his beater and upgrade to a more reliable vehicle.
The beater they borrowed from their neighbor struggled to start in the cold mornings.
She affectionately referred to her worn-out car as her trusty beater.
Despite its age, the beater still managed to chug along on their camping trips.

sense.definition: an old car that is in poor condition and often unreliable

examples.x: He drove a clunker to work every day, its engine coughing and sputtering along the way.
The family finally decided to sell their clunker after it broke down for the third time in a month.
She bought a used bicycle to replace her clunker, which was costing too much in repairs.
The mechanic shook his head sadly as he examined the clunker, noting the extensive rust and worn-out tires.
Despite its age, the clunker held sentimental value for him because it was his first car.

sense.definition: an old, dilapidated car in poor condition

examples.x: They drove around town in a beat-up jalopy during college.
She bought a jalopy for a bargain price and fixed it up.
The jalopy barely made it up the hill due to its worn-out engine.
Driving a jalopy can be a nostalgic experience.
They restored the jalopy to its former glory with new paint and upholstery.

sense.definition: an old, beat-up car that is not in good shape

examples.x: Their family used to have a junker that always broke down on long trips.
The junker in the driveway was covered in rust and missing a side mirror.
He bought a cheap junker to practice fixing cars before starting his mechanic training.
We had to push the junker to get it started every morning.
The junker finally gave out and had to be towed away for scrap metal.

sense.definition: a badly damaged building, ship, car, etc.
the waste gases or air expelled from an engine, furnace, or other machinery

examples.x: After a long journey, the truck emitted a plume of exhaust as it idled in the parking lot.
The factory installed air filters to reduce the release of particulate matter in the exhaust expelled into the atmosphere.
The environmental impact assessment included an analysis of potential effects from industrial exhaust on nearby ecosystems.
Residents raised concerns about the construction site's impact on air quality due to the heavy machinery's exhaust.

sense.definition: the remains of something that has been severely damaged or destroyed, especially after a disaster or accident
a location where various old, damaged items such as vehicles, machinery, and other items are collected, stored, and often sold for parts or recycled

examples.x: The old car was taken to the junkyard after it broke down and couldn't be repaired.
Local children loved to explore the abandoned junkyard, imagining themselves as adventurers in a post-apocalyptic world.
The mechanic found a rare antique engine part buried in the depths of the junkyard, much to his excitement.
Environmental activists protested the expansion of the junkyard, citing concerns about pollution and its impact on the surrounding area.
After salvaging usable parts from the old appliances, the junkyard sold the remaining scrap metal to recycling companies.

sense.definition: a place where old or broken vehicles and machinery are kept and taken apart to sell the usable parts or recycle the materials
a tire of a car, bike, etc. that has been deflated
